forecast.miss_summary: The explicit range forecast got TR and board direction broadly right, but overcounted Codex's contingent hand VP, understated John's card-VP upper tail, and failed to stress-test Banker against late Earth/building production bursts.

key_turning_points.title: Funded Banker from a large current lead without stress-testing John's plausible production burst
Called hard close without proving the earliest feasible finish generation or required VP floor
Counted held and conditional card VP inside the expected scoring floor
John converted the unavoidable final action phase into Jovian multipliers and accumulator VP

counterfactuals.change: Do not fund Banker unless the lead survives a two-card production burst based on John's visible Earth and building tags
Split the Generation 6 card forecast into locked and contingent VP and require a 15-point locked card/city floor for a Generation 11 close
By Generation 8, assign a funded source to every remaining global step and build one additional early oxygen conversion to make a Generation 10 finish credible
Add a 10-20 VP hidden-multiplier reserve once John has four effective Jovian tags plus strong card velocity

rule_updates: If the macro call is hard close, then write the earliest feasible finish generation and assign a source to every remaining global step; if the finish remains Generation 11 or later against John, add a locked VP and denial plan.
If forecasting card VP, then separate locked VP already played or fully funded from contingent VP in hand and use only locked plus budgeted VP to judge the position.
If funding Banker against a draw and discount engine, then stress-test the lead against the opponent's best plausible two-card production burst from visible tags.
If the opponent has four effective Jovian tags plus strong draw or space discounts, then add 10-20 hidden VP to the value of one extra generation.
If the opponent has an unused stealing accumulator, then do not add a vulnerable resource unless it can be converted before the opponent's next action.
If buying a card in Generation 10 or later, then include its exact play action and payment in the liquidation script or do not buy it.

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: Hard-close commitment by Generation 8 performs better than hybrid drift against John's science engine.
active_hypotheses.result: Codex pivoted in Generation 3 and finished in Generation 11, but hard close without a proven finish date and locked VP floor still lost the card-VP race 10-44.

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: Early city anchors can offset or prevent a card-VP deficit.
active_hypotheses.result: Two cities produced 6 VP and kept total board VP even, but could not offset a 34-point card-VP deficit.

active_hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuing actions after terraforming completion materially changes outcomes.
active_hypotheses.result: Codex correctly continued, added Security Fleet VP, exhausted useful actions, and placed a final greenery.

endgame_review.best_missed_conversion: No material final-action conversion was missed; the measurable late inefficiency was buying Solar Power for 3 MC and later selling it for 1 MC without playing it.

tool_mismatches.tool: mutating MCP calls with opponent polling
tool_mismatches.expected: Return promptly after a successful mutation.
tool_mismatches.actual: Several calls remained open while John resolved actions, although the mutations had committed.
tool_mismatches.mitigation: Inspect live state before retrying any timed-out or long-running mutation; this caused no VP loss.
